l1: Question 1 — Is the slowness affecting one user or multiple users?
Question 2 — Is this on a Windows Server or a Windows 10/11 desktop?
Question 3 — Is the machine an on-premises physical box or a cloud virtual machine?
Question 4 — What are the specifications of the affected machine?
Question 5 — Is this isolated to a single machine or widespread?
Question 6 — What specific process (EXE) is driving the load?
Question 7 — What is the exact process name listed at the top of Task Manager?
Question 8 — Is the high CPU constant or spiking?
Question 9 — Is the consumption happening in User Mode or Kernel Mode?
Question 10 — When did the high CPU usage begin?
Question 11 — Does it happen during specific activities?
Question 12 — Does a reboot temporarily resolve the issue?
Question 13 — Were there any recent software deployments or code updates?
Question 14 — Has Windows Update run recently?
Question 15 — Have any hardware, driver, or BIOS changes occurred?
l2: Is the slowness affecting one user or multiple users?
What is the exact process name listed at the top of Task Manager?
Does it happen during specific activities?
Does a reboot temporarily resolve the issue?
Is the consumption happening in User Mode or Kernel Mode?
Is this isolated to a single machine or widespread?

l1: Question 1 - What specific process name (EXE) is listed at the top of the Task Manager CPU column?
Question 2 - Is the CPU usage locked at a flat 100%, or does it rapidly spike and drop?
Question 3 - When you look at the Task Manager Performance tab, is the high usage driven by Logical processors or Kernel times?
Question 4 - Is the high CPU accompanied by high disk activity (100% Active Time) or high memory usage?
Question 5 - Does the CPU spike immediately upon booting up, or does it build up gradually over several hours?
Question 6 - Does this behavior occur only when specific actions are taken?
Question 7 - Is the affected machine a physical on-premises box, or is it a virtual machine (VM) in the cloud or a local hypervisor?
Question 8 - What are the basic resource specifications assigned to this machine?
l2: Is the slowness bound to a specific time of day?
If you temporarily terminate the offending process or restart the machine, how quickly does the high CPU usage return?
Were there any software updates, application deployments, or code changes pushed right before this started?
Has Windows Update run or failed to install a patch recently?
Have there been any recent hardware modifications, driver updates, or BIOS/firmware flashes?
Is this issue isolated to a single machine/user, or are multiple endpoints experiencing the exact same symptom?

l1: Identify the Culprit via Task Manager.
Check for High Kernel Times (Driver/Hardware Issues).
End the Runaway Process Safely.
Perform a Clean boot.
Check Windows Update and Native Services.
Disable Demanding Startup Applications.
Check the behavior in safe mode
l2: Deep-Dive Threads via Sysinternals Process Explorer.
Troubleshoot Hardware Interrupts & DPCs (Deferred Procedure Calls).
Analyze CPU Bottlenecks with Performance Monitor.
Check Event Viewer for Application & System Crashes.
Isolate the process in svchost container.

l1: Collect Performance monitor logs.
Collect WPR logs.
Grab FLTMC output.
l2: Collect Process dump if the process is continously spiking.
Collect complete dump if required (Not required usually).

steps.action: Run Performance Monitor on affected machine.
steps.expect: Identify the process in Perfomance Monitor logs.

steps.action: Check Task Manager to observe the process taking high cpu.
steps.expect: Confirm the behavior of the process how frequently it is consuming.

steps.action: End the process in task manager.
steps.expect: CPU utilization should be normal once it is ended.

steps.action: Check the behavior in Safe Mode.
steps.expect: In safe Mode the issue should not be present.

steps.action: Check if the process is 3rd party.
steps.expect: Uninstall the application related to the process and mention to customer to reach out to vendor.
We should see normal behavior in logs.

steps.action: Monitor the machine for a while.
